content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Los Angeles, California 90017

A-75-07-003

July 14, 1975

Mr. George G. Hardie
Golden State Greyhound Association
925 L Street, Suite 370
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Hardie:

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-143

Thank you for your letter of July 27, 1975 requesting an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding ballot initiative expenditures.

Your request for a formal opinion is denied for the following reasons:

(1) The issues raised in your first two questions have been noticed for consideration at the August 4-7 meeting of the Commission. You are encouraged to present your views regarding these issues to the Commission at that meeting.

(2) The third question you raise, regarding the spending of an individual's private funds to gather signatures, is answered by Section 82015 of the Act which states that the definition of "contribution"

... does not include volunteer personal services or payments made by any individual for his own travel expenses if such payments are made voluntarily without any understanding or agreement that they shall be, directly or indirectly, repaid to him.

However, all other expenditures made by an individual must be reported and would be subject to the overall limitation.

(4) Your last question, regarding a private individual's expenditures to support his views in the media is answered by Section 82200(b) of the Act, which states that "expenditure" does not include:

Expenditures for advertising or speech regarding the measure unless the advertising or speech is directly incidental to circulation of the petition.

Thomas G. Hardin
July 14, 1975
Page Two


Although no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the
foregoing is sufficient to warrant. If you have any questions,
I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Daniel R. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DRL:jp

Request No. 75 148                          George G. Hardie
                                                      PRESIDENT
Data Received 6-30-75                                 Los Angeles (213) 632-3114
Response Due 7-14-75                                  San Diego (714) 299-1547

GOLDEN STATE GREYHOUND ASSOCIATION
Main Office: 925 L Street, Suite 270, Sacramento, CA 95814 (916) 441-7502 / 441-7503


July 27, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95815

Gentlemen:

     Our organization has been compelled by the legis-
lature to seek redress from the public. Knowing we will
be operating under an aura of strange and new rules and
their interpretations concerning ballot initiatives, I
would like to get answers to the following questions.

     The maximum allowed expenditure of 25¢ per valid
signature plus cost of living increases -- when
does the cost of living increase take effect?
     How will you separate institutional advertising as
opposed to ballot initiative advertising?
     To what extent may a private individual spend his
own funds to gather signatures?
     To what extent may a private individual support
his views on a ballot initiative in the media?

     You gentlemen continually take pride in stating the
fact that you helped draft Proposition #9. Why then, is
there such a problem in defining its meaning. We are
beginning our signature drive approximately September 1st.
I need the answers to these questions NOW. Please inform
me when you plan to discuss the above issues so that I may
be in attendance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        George G. Hardie

dp

REGIONAL OFFICES: LOS ANGELES, SAN JOSE, FRESNO, ORANGE COUNTY, SAN DIEGO

Hardie Golden State Greyhound Association 925 L Street, Suite 270 Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Mr. Hardie: Re: Request for Opinion No. 75-143 Thank you for your letter of July 27, 1975 requesting an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding ballot initiative expenditures. Your request for a formal opinion is denied for the following reasons: (1) The issues raised in your first two questions have been noticed for consideration at the August 6-7 meeting of the Commission. You are encouraged to present your views regarding these issues to the Commission at that meeting. (2) The third question you raise, regarding the spending of an individual's private funds to gather signatures, is answered by Section 82015 of the Act which states that the definition of "contribution" ... does not include volunteer personal services or payments made by any individual for his own travel expenses if such payments are made voluntarily without any understanding or agreement that they shall be, directly or indirectly, repaid to him. However, all other expenditures made by an individual must be reported and would be subject to the overall limitation. (4) Your last question, regarding a private individual's expenditures to support his views in the media is answered by Section 35200(b) of the Act, which states that "expenditure" does not include: Expenditures for advertising or speech regarding the measure unless the advertising or speech is directly incidental to circulation of the petition. Although no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you have any questions, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff. Sincerely, [signature] Daniel H.
